title: calamagrostis neglecta subsp. groenlandica
intro: Calamagrostis neglecta subsp. groenlandica, also known as Arctic fescue, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is native to the Arctic regions of North America and Greenland. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin infections. The herbal preparations of Arctic fescue include tea, infusion, and poultice, which are used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
